 `./worktrees/match-number-improvements/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-10-match-number-improvements-design.md`

```text
1 # MatchNumber Game Improvements Design
2 
3 ## Overview
4 
5 Five improvements to the MatchNumber game targeting young readers and fixing
6 existing mode bugs.
7 
8 ## 1. Domino Tile Overhaul (Uniform Size + Vertical Orientation + Simpler Splits)
9 
10 ### Problem
11 
12 - Dice tiles (1–6) are square 80×80, domino tiles (7–12) are wide rectangles
13   128×72 — inconsistent sizing
14 - Dominoes render horizontally (left/right halves) — unnatural for a domino
15 - Splits are not optimised for young readers (e.g. 10 = 6+4 instead of 5+5)
16 - Pips are too close to the divider line
17 
18 ### Solution
19 
20 Unify all `dots`-style tiles into a single vertical domino shape.
21 
22 **New `DOMINO_SPLIT` map (top/bottom, smaller value on top):**
23 
24 | Value | Top | Bottom |
25 | ----- | --- | ------ |
26 | 1     | 1   | 0      |
27 | 2     | 2   | 0      |
28 | 3     | 3   | 0      |
29 | 4     | 4   | 0      |
30 | 5     | 5   | 0      |
31 | 6     | 6   | 0      |
32 | 7     | 2   | 5      |
33 | 8     | 4   | 4      |
34 | 9     | 4   | 5      |
35 | 10    | 5   | 5      |
36 | 11    | 5   | 6      |
37 | 12    | 6   | 6      |
38 
39 A bottom value of `0` means blank half (no pips).
40 
41 **Component changes:**
42 
43 - Replace separate `DiceFace`/`DominoTile` exports with a single `DominoTile`
44   component that always renders vertically
45 - `DiceFace` becomes internal-only (renders one half of the domino)
46 - All tiles: `w-[72px] h-[136px]` (tall rectangle)
47 - Horizontal divider line, shorter than tile width (`w-11`), `opacity-30`
48 - Generous padding (`py-3`) between pip grids and divider
49 - Remove `allDice` branching logic in `NumberMatch.tsx` and
50   `NumeralTileBank.tsx` — when `tileStyle === 'dots'`, every tile/slot/hole
51   uses the same domino dimensions
52 
53 ## 2. Mode Bug Fix + New Modes
54 
55 ### Problem
56 
57 - `group-to-numeral`: tiles render as dominoes instead of plain numerals
58 - `numeral-to-word` / `word-to-numeral`: tiles show plain numbers, no word
59   conversion exists
60 - `tileStyle` options `objects` and `fingers` are stubs (fall through to numeral
61   text) — keep them as stubs for future work
62 
63 ### Solution
64 
65 **Rendering gate:** Introduce a derived boolean `tilesShowGroup` that is `true`
66 only when `mode === 'numeral-to-group'`. Domino rendering only applies when
67 `tilesShowGroup && tileStyle === 'dots'`.
68 
69 This boolean gates rendering in three places:
70 
71 1. `NumeralTile` component
72 2. `Slot` render callback in `NumberMatch.tsx`
73 3. Bank hole hover preview
74 
75 `NumeralTileBank` receives a new prop `tilesShowGroup: boolean`.
76 
77 **Slot dimensions:**
78 
79 - `tilesShowGroup && tileStyle === 'dots'` → portrait `w-[72px] h-[136px]`
80 - All other modes → square `size-20`
81 
82 **8 game modes:**
83 
84 | Mode                      | Question  | Answer    | Conversion   |
85 | ------------------------- | --------- | --------- | ------------ |
86 | `numeral-to-group`        | 7         | Domino    | —            |
87 | `group-to-numeral`        | Dots      | 7         | —            |
88 | `cardinal-number-to-text` | 7         | "seven"   | `toCardinal` |
89 | `cardinal-text-to-number` | "seven"   | 7         | `toCardinal` |
90 | `ordinal-number-to-text`  | "7th"     | "seventh" | `toOrdinal`  |
91 | `ordinal-text-to-number`  | "seventh" | "7th"     | `toOrdinal`  |
92 | `cardinal-to-ordinal`     | "seven"   | "seventh" | both         |
93 | `ordinal-to-cardinal`     | "seventh" | "seven"   | both         |
94 
95 **Dependency:** `n2words` (only) — tree-shakeable per-locale imports, i18n, zero
96 deps. Provides `toCardinal` and `toOrdinal` per locale. No additional ordinal
97 suffix packages needed — the numeric ordinal format ("7th" / "7º") is trivial
98 inline logic.
99 
100 **Utility module:** `src/games/number-match/number-words.ts`
101 
102 - `toCardinalText(n, locale)` → `"seven"` / `"sete"` — wraps
103   `n2words/<locale>.toCardinal`
104 - `toOrdinalText(n, locale)` → `"seventh"` / `"sétimo"` — wraps
105   `n2words/<locale>.toOrdinal`
106 - `toOrdinalNumber(n, locale)` → `"7th"` / `"7º"` — inline suffix logic
107   (English: 1st/2nd/3rd/Nth; Portuguese: always `º`)
108 - Locale map: `'en'` → `n2words/en-AU`, `'pt-BR'` → `n2words/pt-PT`
109 
110 **Data flow:** Rounds are always generated with numeric values. At render time,
111 the utility converts to display strings based on the mode. Matching logic always
112 compares the underlying numeric `value`, never text strings.
113 
114 **Locale:** Sourced from the existing `useParams({ from: '/$locale' })` router
115 param (`'en'` | `'pt-BR'`). The utility maps app locales to `n2words` locale
116 imports.
117 
118 **Word tile UI:**
119 
120 - Square tiles with `min-width: 80px`, height 80px
121 - Font size scales down for longer words
122 - Long words break at hyphens across two lines
123 - Slots and bank holes match using `min-width`
124 
125 ### Types update
126 
127 ```typescript
128 type NumberMatchMode =
129   | 'numeral-to-group'
130   | 'group-to-numeral'
131   | 'cardinal-number-to-text'
132   | 'cardinal-text-to-number'
133   | 'ordinal-number-to-text'
134   | 'ordinal-text-to-number'
135   | 'cardinal-to-ordinal'
136   | 'ordinal-to-cardinal';
137 ```
138 
139 ## 3. Countable Dots on DotGroupQuestion
140 
141 ### Problem
142 
143 Young kids need to count dots but have no way to track which dots they have
144 already counted.
145 
146 ### Solution
147 
148 Each dot in `DotGroupQuestion` becomes individually tappable:
149 
150 - Tap an unnumbered dot → assigns the next increment (1, 2, 3...)
151 - The number appears centered on the dot
152 - Each tap triggers TTS speaking the cardinal word ("one", "two", "three"...)
153 - Already-tapped dots do nothing on re-tap (no toggle)
154 - Counts auto-reset when `count` changes (next round)
155 - Order does not matter — kids can tap dots in any sequence
156 - TTS for the whole question area stays on the existing `AudioButton`
157 
158 **State:** Local `useState` inside `DotGroupQuestion` — array of
159 `(number | null)` per dot, tracking assigned count values. A `nextCount` ref
160 tracks the next number to assign.
161 
162 **Rendering:** Each dot changes from a plain `<span>` to a `<button>`. When
163 tapped, the dot shows a white number centered on the primary-colored circle.
164 
165 ## 4. Hover Preview Fix
166 
167 ### Problem
168 
169 When dragging a tile over a slot or bank hole, the preview shows plain text
170 label (e.g. "7") instead of the domino face.
171 
172 ### Solution
173 
174 The hover preview renders the same visual as the tile:
175 
176 - Domino face when `tilesShowGroup && tileStyle === 'dots'`
177 - Plain text otherwise
178 
179 Affected locations:
180 
181 1. **Slot preview** — the `Slot` render callback uses `previewLabel` to render
182    a domino or text
183 2. **Bank hole preview** — the empty hole in `NumeralTileBank` renders domino or
184    text during hover
185 
186 Both reuse the existing `DominoTile` / `DiceFace` components.
187 
188 ## Files Affected
189 
190 - `src/games/number-match/types.ts` — mode type expansion, config field options
191 - `src/games/number-match/NumberMatch/NumberMatch.tsx` — rendering logic,
192   slot sizing, mode gating
193 - `src/games/number-match/NumeralTileBank/NumeralTileBank.tsx` — domino
194   overhaul, tile rendering, hover preview, `tilesShowGroup` prop
195 - `src/games/number-match/number-words.ts` — number↔text conversion utility
196   wrapping `n2words`
197 - `src/games/number-match/number-words.test.ts` — unit tests for the utility
198 - `src/games/number-match/build-numeral-round.ts` — label generation for
199   word/ordinal modes
200 - `src/components/questions/DotGroupQuestion/DotGroupQuestion.tsx` — countable
201   dots, per-dot tap, TTS
202 - `src/games/number-match/NumberMatch/NumberMatch.stories.tsx` — new stories
203   for all modes
204 - `src/games/number-match/NumeralTileBank/NumeralTileBank.stories.tsx` — updated
205   stories
206 - `package.json` — add `n2words` dependency
207 
208 ## Out of Scope
209 
210 - `objects` and `fingers` tile styles — kept as stubs
211 - Drag preview ghost image (the floating tile during drag) — separate concern
212
```

36

38 const toCardinalText: (n: number, locale: AppLocale) => string;
39

### 3. **Type Definitions from `src/games/number-match/types.ts`**

Key types:

- **`NumberMatchRound`**: `{ value: number; objectImage?: string }`
- **`NumberMatchConfig extends AnswerGameConfig`**:
  - `component: 'NumberMatch'`
  - `mode: 'numeral-to-group' | 'group-to-numeral' | 'numeral-to-word' | 'word-to-numeral'`
  - `tileStyle: 'dots' | 'objects' | 'fingers'`
  - `range: { min: number; max: number }`
  - `rounds: NumberMatchRound[]`

Config fields include: `inputMethod`, `wrongTileBehavior`, `mode`, `tileStyle`, `tileBankMode`, `distractorCount`, `totalRounds`, `roundsInOrder`, `range` (min/max), `ttsEnabled`.

### 4. **NumberMatch.tsx Component Structure**

**Key Props**: `config: NumberMatchConfig`, `initialState?: AnswerGameDraftState`, `sessionId?: string`, `seed?: string`

**How it handles `allDice` (lines 126-132)**:
```typescript
const allDice = allTiles.every((t) => {
  const n = Number.parseInt(t.value, 10);
  return !Number.isNaN(n) && n <= 6;
});
const slotClass = allDice
  ? 'size-20 rounded-2xl'
  : 'h-[72px] w-32 rounded-2xl';
```

Checks if all tiles have numeric values ≤ 6 to determine slot sizing. Dice (1–6) get small squares; dominoes/larger tiles get wider slots.

**Slot Rendering (lines 154–177)**:

- Renders `<Slot>` components with a render callback `({ label })`
- Inside the callback, checks `tileStyle === 'dots'` and numeric value to decide:
  - If `n <= 6`: renders `<DiceFace value={n} />`
  - If `n > 6`: renders `<DominoTile value={n} />`
  - Otherwise: renders plain text `<span>{label}</span>`

**Mode Gating (lines 134–150)**:

- `showTextQuestion` is true for `'numeral-to-group'`, `'numeral-to-word'`, `'word-to-numeral'`
- Renders `<DotGroupQuestion>` for `'group-to-numeral'` mode
- Renders `<TextQuestion>` for other modes

**Tile Rendering**: All tile rendering is delegated to the slot callback — the component doesn't directly render `DiceFace` or `DominoTile`, only instructs slots how to render based on label parsing.

### 5. **NumeralTileBank.tsx Component Structure**

**Props**: `tileStyle: TileStyle` (`'dots' | 'objects' | 'fingers'`)

**How it handles dice vs. domino tiles (lines 9–13, 88–89)**:

```typescript
const getIsDomino = (tileStyle: TileStyle, numericValue: number) =>
  tileStyle === 'dots' &&
  !Number.isNaN(numericValue) &&
  numericValue > 6 &&
  numericValue <= 12;
```

Domino detection: only in `'dots'` mode, values 7–12.

**How it handles `allDice` branching**:

- Uses `useAnswerGameContext()` to access `allTiles`, `bankTileIds`, `dragActiveTileId`, `dragHoverBankTileId`
- For each tile, computes `isDomino` dynamically
- Applies different sizing: `isDomino ? 'h-[72px] w-32' : 'size-20'`

**Hover Previews (lines 187–192, 212–215)**:

- When `isHoverTarget` is true, renders a dashed border + semi-transparent preview
- Shows `tile.label` on empty holes being previewed

**Tile Bank Rendering (lines 160–219)**:

- Tiles in bank: renders `<NumeralTile>` wrapper with hole background
- Tiles in slots: shows empty hole with optional preview label when drag-hovering

**NumeralTile sub-component (lines 71–123)**:

- Uses `useDraggableTile()` for drag interaction
- Renders `<DiceFace>` for `n ≤ 6`, `<DominoTile>` for `7 ≤ n ≤ 12`, plain text otherwise
- Has size classes: `size-20` for dice, `h-[72px] w-32` for dominoes

### 6. **DotGroupQuestion.tsx Component Structure**

**Props**: `count: number`, `prompt: string`

**Current Rendering (lines 21–27)**:

```typescript
{Array.from({ length: count }, (_, i) => (
  <span
    key={i}
    role="presentation"
    className="size-10 rounded-full bg-primary"
  />
))}
```

Simple circle dots (`size-10 rounded-full bg-primary`). Wrapped in a button with `useGameTTS()` for audio playback. Renders flex-wrapped dots centered.

### 7. **build-numeral-round.ts — Round Generation**

**Function**: `buildNumeralRound(value: number, bank: NumeralBankOptions)`

**Returns**: `{ tiles: TileItem[]; zones: AnswerZone[] }`

**Labels Used**:

- Single zone with `expectedValue: String(value)`
- Tiles created with `label: String(n)`, `value: String(n)` for each numeral
- Only numeric string labels (`'1'`, `'2'`, etc.)

**How it generates**:

1. Creates one zone (answer slot) with `expectedValue = String(value)`
2. Builds numeral array: `[value]` + distractors (if `tileBankMode === 'distractors'`)
3. Shuffles and maps to `TileItem[]` with numeric labels and values
4. Returns both arrays

### 8. **Story Files in number-match Directory**

**NumberMatch.stories.tsx**:

- 4 stories: `NumeralToGroup`, `GroupToNumeral`, `NumeralToWord`, `WordToNumeral`
- Each tests different `mode` combinations with various `tileStyle` values

**NumeralTileBank.stories.tsx**:

- 4 stories: `Default` (dots), `FingersStyle`, `ObjectsStyle`, `DragHoverBankTile`
- Uses `AnswerGameProvider` with mock tiles (1–5) and zones

**DotGroupQuestion.stories.tsx**:

- 3 stories: `OneDot`, `ThreeDots`, `FiveDots`
- Tests different dot counts with TTS integration

### 9. **DiceFace and DominoTile Components**

**Location**: Exported from `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/match-number-improvements/src/games/number-match/NumeralTileBank/NumeralTileBank.tsx` (lines 35–69)

**DiceFace Component**:

```typescript
const DiceFace = ({ value }: { value: number }) => {
  const pips = DICE_PIPS[value] ?? [];
  return (
    <div className="grid grid-cols-3 grid-rows-3 gap-1 p-1" aria-hidden="true">
      {/* 9-cell grid, filled/empty based on pip positions */}
    </div>
  );
};
```

- Uses `DICE_PIPS` lookup table (positions 0–8 for each die value 1–6)
- Renders 3×3 grid of dots, fills pip positions based on die value
- Supports: 1 (center), 2 (corners), 3 (diagonal), 4 (four corners), 5 (five including center), 6 (all but center cross)

**DominoTile Component**:

```typescript
const DominoTile = ({ value }: { value: number }) => {
  const [left, right] = DOMINO_SPLIT[value] ?? [6, value - 6];
  return (
    <div className="flex items-center gap-0" aria-hidden="true">
      <DiceFace value={left} />
      <span className="h-10 w-px shrink-0 bg-current opacity-30" />
      <DiceFace value={right} />
    </div>
  );
};
```

- Uses `DOMINO_SPLIT` to decompose values 7–12 into two die values
- Renders two `DiceFace` components side-by-side with a vertical divider
- Domino splits: 7→[4,3], 8→[4,4], 9→[5,4], 10→[6,4], 11→[6,5], 12→[6,6]

**Exports**: Both are named exports from `NumeralTileBank.tsx` (lines 8 in NumberMatch.tsx imports them)

### 10. **Slot Component and previewLabel Logic**

**Location**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/match-number-improvements/src/components/answer-game/Slot/Slot.tsx` and `useSlotBehavior.ts`

**Slot.tsx** (lines 13–145):

- Takes `children: (props: SlotRenderProps) => ReactNode` render callback
- Receives `renderProps` from `useSlotBehavior()` which includes:
  - `label: string | null` — the placed tile's label
  - `previewLabel: string | null` — what the slot will receive on drop
  - `isPreview: boolean` — true if dragging over or dragging from this slot

**previewLabel Logic** (useSlotBehavior.ts, lines 80–103):

```typescript
let isPreview = false;
let previewLabel: string | null = null;

if (isPreviewTarget) {
  // Slot is the drop target — show the dragged tile's label
  isPreview = true;
  previewLabel = draggedTile?.label ?? null;
} else if (isBeingDragged && dragHoverZoneIndex !== null) {
  // Slot is the source — show what it will receive (target's tile)
  isPreview = true;
  const targetZone = zones[dragHoverZoneIndex];
  const targetTile = targetZone?.placedTileId ? ... : null;
  previewLabel = targetTile?.label ?? null; // null means slot becomes empty
}
```

**Slot Rendering with previewLabel** (Slot.tsx, lines 90–141):

- If `isEmpty && isPreview && previewLabel !== null`: renders `<span className="text-xl font-bold opacity-50">{previewLabel}</span>` (semi-transparent preview)
- If `!isEmpty`: renders a button with placed tile, and if `isPreview && previewLabel !== null`: overrides with preview label at opacity-50 on drag-over
- The `children` render callback is skipped when previewing — slot shows the preview label instead

**Key: NumberMatch.tsx never directly uses `previewLabel`** — it only passes the slot's render callback `({ label })` which reads the placed tile's label, not the preview. The preview is handled entirely by Slot's internal logic and renders before the callback.

---

## Summary

This is a comprehensive answer-game system for matching numerals to groups (dots/dominoes), with:

- **Type-safe config** for 4 game modes and 3 visual styles
- **Smart tile rendering**: DiceFace for 1–6, DominoTile for 7–12, fallback to text
- **Drag-and-drop**: With preview labels showing what will land on drop
- **DotGroupQuestion**: Simple circular dot renderer for visual matching prompts
- **Slot-based answer zones**: Support for free-swap and ordered interactions
- **TTS integration**: Voice prompts for accessibility

All file paths are absolute and ready for implementation reference.

```

1 import { useNavigate, useParams } from '@tanstack/react-router';
2 import { useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from 'react';
3 import { buildNumeralRound } from '../build-numeral-round';
4 import {
5   DiceFace,
6   DominoTile,
7   NumeralTileBank,
8 } from '../NumeralTileBank/NumeralTileBank';
9 import type { NumberMatchConfig } from '../types';
10 import type {
11   AnswerGameConfig,
12   AnswerGameDraftState,
13   AnswerZone,
14   TileItem,
15 } from '@/components/answer-game/types';
16 import { AnswerGame } from '@/components/answer-game/AnswerGame/AnswerGame';
17 import { GameOverOverlay } from '@/components/answer-game/GameOverOverlay/GameOverOverlay';
18 import { ScoreAnimation } from '@/components/answer-game/ScoreAnimation/ScoreAnimation';
19 import { Slot } from '@/components/answer-game/Slot/Slot';
20 import { SlotRow } from '@/components/answer-game/Slot/SlotRow';
21 import { useAnswerGameContext } from '@/components/answer-game/useAnswerGameContext';
22 import { useAnswerGameDispatch } from '@/components/answer-game/useAnswerGameDispatch';
23 import { useGameSounds } from '@/components/answer-game/useGameSounds';
24 import { useRoundTTS } from '@/components/answer-game/useRoundTTS';
25 import { AudioButton } from '@/components/questions/AudioButton/AudioButton';
26 import { DotGroupQuestion } from '@/components/questions/DotGroupQuestion/DotGroupQuestion';
27 import { TextQuestion } from '@/components/questions/TextQuestion/TextQuestion';
28 import { buildRoundOrder } from '@/games/build-round-order';
29 
30 interface NumberMatchProps {
31   config: NumberMatchConfig;
32   initialState?: AnswerGameDraftState;
33   sessionId?: string;
34   seed?: string;
35 }
36 
37 const NumberMatchSession = ({
38   numberMatchConfig,
39   roundOrder,
40   onRestartSession,
41 }: {
42   numberMatchConfig: NumberMatchConfig;
43   roundOrder: readonly number[];
44   onRestartSession: () => void;
45 }) => {
46   const { phase, roundIndex, retryCount, zones, allTiles } =
47     useAnswerGameContext();
48   const dispatch = useAnswerGameDispatch();
49   const { confettiReady, gameOverReady } = useGameSounds();
50   const navigate = useNavigate();
51   const { locale } = useParams({ from: '/$locale' });
52   const completionToken = useRef(0);
53 
54   const configRoundIndex = roundOrder[roundIndex];
55   const round =
56     configRoundIndex === undefined
57       ? undefined
58       : numberMatchConfig.rounds[configRoundIndex];
59 
60   useRoundTTS(String(round?.value ?? ''));
61 
62   const handleHome = () => {
63     void navigate({ to: '/$locale', params: { locale } });
64   };
65 
66   const handlePlayAgain = () => {
67     onRestartSession();
68   };
69 
70   useEffect(() => {
71     if (phase !== 'round-complete' || !confettiReady) return;
72 
73     const token = ++completionToken.current;
74     const delayMs = 750;
75     const timer = globalThis.setTimeout(() => {
76       if (completionToken.current !== token) return;
77 
78       const isLastRound = roundIndex >= roundOrder.length - 1;
79       if (isLastRound) {
80         dispatch({ type: 'COMPLETE_GAME' });
81         return;
82       }
83 
84       const nextConfigIndex = roundOrder[roundIndex + 1];
85       const nextRound =
86         nextConfigIndex === undefined
87           ? undefined
88           : numberMatchConfig.rounds[nextConfigIndex];
89       const value = nextRound?.value;
90       if (value === undefined) {
91         dispatch({ type: 'COMPLETE_GAME' });
92         return;
93       }
94       const { tiles: nextTiles, zones: nextZones } = buildNumeralRound(
95         value,
96         {
97           tileBankMode: numberMatchConfig.tileBankMode,
98           distractorCount: numberMatchConfig.distractorCount,
99           range: numberMatchConfig.range,
100         },
101       );
102       dispatch({
103         type: 'ADVANCE_ROUND',
104         tiles: nextTiles,
105         zones: nextZones,
106       });
107     }, delayMs);
108 
109     return () => {
110       globalThis.clearTimeout(timer);
111     };
112   }, [
113     phase,
114     confettiReady,
115     roundIndex,
116     dispatch,
117     roundOrder,
118     numberMatchConfig.rounds,
119     numberMatchConfig.tileBankMode,
120     numberMatchConfig.distractorCount,
121     numberMatchConfig.range,
122   ]);
123 
124   if (!round) return null;
125 
126   const allDice = allTiles.every((t) => {
127     const n = Number.parseInt(t.value, 10);
128     return !Number.isNaN(n) && n <= 6;
129   });
130   const slotClass = allDice
131     ? 'size-20 rounded-2xl'
132     : 'h-[72px] w-32 rounded-2xl';
133 
134   const showTextQuestion =
135     numberMatchConfig.mode === 'numeral-to-group' ||
136     numberMatchConfig.mode === 'numeral-to-word' ||
137     numberMatchConfig.mode === 'word-to-numeral';
138 
139   return (
140     <>
141       <div className="flex w-full max-w-2xl flex-col items-center justify-center gap-8 px-4 py-6">
142         <AnswerGame.Question>
143           {showTextQuestion ? (
144             <TextQuestion text={String(round.value)} />
145           ) : (
146             <DotGroupQuestion
147               count={round.value}
148               prompt={String(round.value)}
149             />
150           )}
151           <AudioButton prompt={String(round.value)} />
152         </AnswerGame.Question>
153         <AnswerGame.Answer>
154           <SlotRow className="gap-4">
155             {zones.map((zone, i) => (
156               <Slot key={zone.id} index={i} className={slotClass}>
157                 {({ label }) => {
158                   if (!label) return null;
159                   const n = Number.parseInt(label, 10);
160                   if (
161                     numberMatchConfig.tileStyle === 'dots' &&
162                     !Number.isNaN(n)
163                   ) {
164                     return n <= 6 ? (
165                       <DiceFace value={n} />
166                     ) : (
167                       <DominoTile value={n} />
168                     );
169                   }
170                   return (
171                     <span className="text-3xl font-bold tabular-nums">
172                       {label}
173                     </span>
174                   );
175                 }}
176               </Slot>
177             ))}
178           </SlotRow>
179         </AnswerGame.Answer>
180         <AnswerGame.Choices>
181           <NumeralTileBank tileStyle={numberMatchConfig.tileStyle} />
182         </AnswerGame.Choices>
183       </div>
184       <ScoreAnimation visible={confettiReady} />
185       {gameOverReady ? (
186         <GameOverOverlay
187           retryCount={retryCount}
188           onPlayAgain={handlePlayAgain}
189           onHome={handleHome}
190         />
191       ) : null}
192     </>
193   );
194 };
195 
196 export const NumberMatch = ({
197   config,
198   initialState,
199   sessionId,
200   seed,
201 }: NumberMatchProps) => {
202   const roundsInOrder = config.roundsInOrder === true;
203   const [sessionEpoch, setSessionEpoch] = useState(0);
204 
205   const roundOrder = useMemo(() => {
206     void sessionEpoch;
207     return buildRoundOrder(config.rounds.length, roundsInOrder, seed);
208   }, [config.rounds.length, roundsInOrder, seed, sessionEpoch]);
209 
210   const firstConfigIndex = roundOrder[0];
211   const round0 =
212     firstConfigIndex === undefined
213       ? undefined
214       : config.rounds[firstConfigIndex];
215   const roundValue = round0?.value;
216 
217   const { tiles, zones } = useMemo(() => {
218     if (roundValue === undefined) {
219       return { tiles: [] as TileItem[], zones: [] as AnswerZone[] };
220     }
221     return buildNumeralRound(roundValue, {
222       tileBankMode: config.tileBankMode,
223       distractorCount: config.distractorCount,
224       range: config.range,
225     });
226   }, [
227     roundValue,
228     config.tileBankMode,
229     config.distractorCount,
230     config.range,
231   ]);
232 
233   const answerGameConfig = useMemo(
234     (): AnswerGameConfig => ({
235       gameId: config.gameId,
236       inputMethod: config.inputMethod,
237       wrongTileBehavior: config.wrongTileBehavior,
238       tileBankMode: config.tileBankMode,
239       distractorCount: config.distractorCount,
240       totalRounds: config.rounds.length,
241       roundsInOrder: config.roundsInOrder,
242       ttsEnabled: config.ttsEnabled,
243       initialTiles: tiles,
244       initialZones: zones,
245       slotInteraction: 'free-swap' as const,
246     }),
247     [
248       config.gameId,
249       config.inputMethod,
250       config.wrongTileBehavior,
251       config.tileBankMode,
252       config.distractorCount,
253       config.rounds.length,
254       config.roundsInOrder,
255       config.ttsEnabled,
256       tiles,
257       zones,
258     ],
259   );
260 
261   if (!round0) return null;
262 
263   return (
264     <AnswerGame
265       config={answerGameConfig}
266       initialState={initialState}
267       sessionId={sessionId}
268     >
269       <NumberMatchSession
270         numberMatchConfig={config}
271         roundOrder={roundOrder}
272         onRestartSession={() => {
273           setSessionEpoch((e) => e + 1);
274         }}
275       />
276     </AnswerGame>
277   );
278 };
279
```

 `./worktrees/match-number-improvements/src/games/number-match/NumeralTileBank/NumeralTileBank.tsx`

```text
1 import type { TileItem } from '@/components/answer-game/types';
2 import { skeuoStyle } from '@/components/answer-game/styles';
3 import { useAnswerGameContext } from '@/components/answer-game/useAnswerGameContext';
4 import { useBankDropTarget } from '@/components/answer-game/useBankDropTarget';
5 import { useDraggableTile } from '@/components/answer-game/useDraggableTile';
6 
7 type TileStyle = 'dots' | 'objects' | 'fingers';
8 
9 const getIsDomino = (tileStyle: TileStyle, numericValue: number) =>
10   tileStyle === 'dots' &&
11   !Number.isNaN(numericValue) &&
12   numericValue > 6 &&
13   numericValue <= 12;
14 
15 /** Pip positions (0–8) for each die value in a 3×3 grid. */
16 const DICE_PIPS: Record<number, number[]> = {
17   1: [4],
18   2: [2, 6],
19   3: [2, 4, 6],
20   4: [0, 2, 6, 8],
21   5: [0, 2, 4, 6, 8],
22   6: [0, 2, 3, 5, 6, 8],
23 };
24 
25 /** Which two die values add up to n for values 7–12. */
26 const DOMINO_SPLIT: Record<number, [number, number]> = {
27   7: [4, 3],
28   8: [4, 4],
29   9: [5, 4],
30   10: [6, 4],
31   11: [6, 5],
32   12: [6, 6],
33 };
34 
35 export const DiceFace = ({ value }: { value: number }) => {
36   const pips = DICE_PIPS[value] ?? [];
37   return (
38     <div
39       className="grid grid-cols-3 grid-rows-3 gap-1 p-1"
40       aria-hidden="true"
41     >
42       {Array.from({ length: 9 }, (_, i) => (
43         <span
44           key={i}
45           data-cell=""
46           data-pip={pips.includes(i) ? '' : undefined}
47           className={[
48             'size-2.5 rounded-full',
49             pips.includes(i) ? 'bg-current' : 'bg-transparent',
50           ].join(' ')}
51         />
52       ))}
53     </div>
54   );
55 };
56 
57 export const DominoTile = ({ value }: { value: number }) => {
58   const [left, right] = DOMINO_SPLIT[value] ?? [6, value - 6];
59   return (
60     <div className="flex items-center gap-0" aria-hidden="true">
61       <DiceFace value={left} />
62       <span
63         data-divider=""
64         className="h-10 w-px shrink-0 bg-current opacity-30"
65       />
66       <DiceFace value={right} />
67     </div>
68   );
69 };
70 
71 const NumeralTile = ({
72   tile,
73   tileStyle,
74 }: {
75   tile: TileItem;
76   tileStyle: TileStyle;
77 }) => {
78   const {
79     ref,
80     handleClick,
81     onPointerDown,
82     onPointerMove,
83     onPointerUp,
84     onPointerCancel,
85   } = useDraggableTile(tile);
86   const numericValue = Number.parseInt(tile.value, 10);
87 
88   const isDomino = getIsDomino(tileStyle, numericValue);
89 
90   return (
91     <button
92       ref={ref}
93       type="button"
94       aria-label={`Number ${tile.label}`}
95       className={[
96         'flex shrink-0 touch-none select-none cursor-grab flex-col items-center justify-center gap-0.5 rounded-2xl p-2 transition-transform active:scale-95 active:cursor-grabbing',
97         isDomino ? 'h-[72px] w-32' : 'size-20',
98       ].join(' ')}
99       style={skeuoStyle}
100       onClick={handleClick}
101       onPointerDown={onPointerDown}
102       onPointerMove={onPointerMove}
103       onPointerUp={onPointerUp}
104       onPointerCancel={onPointerCancel}
105     >
106       {tileStyle === 'dots' && !Number.isNaN(numericValue) ? (
107         numericValue <= 6 ? (
108           <DiceFace value={numericValue} />
109         ) : numericValue <= 12 ? (
110           <DominoTile value={numericValue} />
111         ) : (
112           <span className="text-3xl font-bold tabular-nums leading-none">
113             {tile.label}
114           </span>
115         )
116       ) : (
117         <span className="text-3xl font-bold tabular-nums leading-none">
118           {tile.label}
119         </span>
120       )}
121     </button>
122   );
123 };
124 
125 export interface NumeralTileBankProps {
126   tileStyle: TileStyle;
127 }
128 
129 export const NumeralTileBank = ({
130   tileStyle,
131 }: NumeralTileBankProps) => {
132   const {
133     allTiles,
134     bankTileIds,
135     dragActiveTileId,
136     dragHoverBankTileId,
137   } = useAnswerGameContext();
138   const { bankRef, isDragOver } = useBankDropTarget();
139   return (
140     <div
141       ref={bankRef}
142       data-tile-bank=""
143       className="flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-3"
144     >
145       {allTiles
146         .filter((t) => !t.id.startsWith('typed-'))
147         .map((tile) => {
148           const inBank = bankTileIds.includes(tile.id);
149           const isDragging = tile.id === dragActiveTileId;
150           const isHoverTarget =
151             tile.id === dragHoverBankTileId ||
152             (!dragHoverBankTileId &&
153               isDragOver &&
154               !inBank &&
155               tile.id === dragActiveTileId);
156           const numericValue = Number.parseInt(tile.value, 10);
157           const isDomino = getIsDomino(tileStyle, numericValue);
158           const holeSizeClass = isDomino ? 'h-[72px] w-32' : 'size-20';
159 
160           if (inBank) {
161             return (
162               <div
163                 key={tile.id}
164                 className={[
165                   'relative rounded-2xl transition-all',
166                   holeSizeClass,
167                 ]
168                   .filter(Boolean)
169                   .join(' ')}
170               >
171                 <div
172                   data-tile-bank-hole={tile.id}
173                   className={[
174                     'rounded-2xl bg-muted/60 shadow-inner',
175                     holeSizeClass,
176                   ]
177                     .filter(Boolean)
178                     .join(' ')}
179                   aria-hidden="true"
180                 />
181                 <div
182                   className={`absolute inset-0${isDragging ? ' opacity-30 pointer-events-none' : ''}`}
183                   aria-hidden={isDragging || undefined}
184                 >
185                   <NumeralTile tile={tile} tileStyle={tileStyle} />
186                 </div>
187                 {isHoverTarget && (
188                   <div
189                     className="pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 rounded-2xl border-2 border-dashed border-primary"
190                     aria-hidden="true"
191                   />
192                 )}
193               </div>
194             );
195           }
196 
197           return (
198             <div
199               key={tile.id}
200               data-tile-bank-hole={tile.id}
201               className={[
202                 'relative rounded-2xl bg-muted/60 shadow-inner transition-all',
203                 holeSizeClass,
204                 isHoverTarget
205                   ? 'border-2 border-dashed border-primary'
206                   : '',
207               ]
208                 .filter(Boolean)
209                 .join(' ')}
210               aria-hidden="true"
211             >
212               {isHoverTarget && (
213                 <span className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center text-2xl font-bold opacity-50">
214                   {tile.label}
215                 </span>
216               )}
217             </div>
218           );
219         })}
220     </div>
221   );
222 };
223
```

 `./worktrees/match-number-improvements/src/games/number-match/build-numeral-round.ts`

```text
1 import { nanoid } from 'nanoid';
2 import type {
3   AnswerZone,
4   TileItem,
5 } from '@/components/answer-game/types';
6 
7 export type NumeralBankOptions = {
8   tileBankMode: 'exact' | 'distractors';
9   distractorCount?: number;
10   range: { min: number; max: number };
11 };
12 
13 function shuffleInPlace<T>(items: T[]): void {
14   for (let i = items.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
15     const j = Math.floor(Math.random() * (i + 1));
16     [items[i], items[j]] = [items[j]!, items[i]!];
17   }
18 }
19 
20 /** Distinct integers in [min, max] excluding `correct`, up to `count` values. */
21 export function pickDistractorNumerals(
22   correct: number,
23   count: number,
24   min: number,
25   max: number,
26 ): number[] {
27   if (count <= 0 || min > max) return [];
28   const pool: number[] = [];
29   for (let v = min; v <= max; v++) {
30     if (v !== correct) pool.push(v);
31   }
32   if (pool.length === 0) return [];
33   shuffleInPlace(pool);
34   return pool.slice(0, Math.min(count, pool.length));
35 }
36 
37 /**
38  * One answer zone (expected numeral) plus bank tiles: correct only, or correct + distractors.
39  */
40 export function buildNumeralRound(
41   value: number,
42   bank: NumeralBankOptions,
43 ): { tiles: TileItem[]; zones: AnswerZone[] } {
44   const zone: AnswerZone = {
45     id: nanoid(),
46     index: 0,
47     expectedValue: String(value),
48     placedTileId: null,
49     isWrong: false,
50     isLocked: false,
51   };
52 
53   const numerals: number[] = [value];
54   if (bank.tileBankMode === 'distractors') {
55     const extra = pickDistractorNumerals(
56       value,
57       bank.distractorCount ?? 0,
58       bank.range.min,
59       bank.range.max,
60     );
61     numerals.push(...extra);
62   }
63 
64   shuffleInPlace(numerals);
65   const tiles: TileItem[] = numerals.map((n) => ({
66     id: nanoid(),
67     label: String(n),
68     value: String(n),
69   }));
70 
71   return { tiles, zones: [zone] };
72 }
73
```

</details></tool-use>

1 import { skeuoStyle } from '../styles';
2 import { useSlotBehavior } from './useSlotBehavior';
3 import type { SlotRenderProps } from './useSlotBehavior';
4 import type { ReactNode, Ref } from 'react';
5 
6 interface SlotProps {
7   index: number;
8   as?: 'li' | 'span' | 'div';
9   className?: string;
10   children: (props: SlotRenderProps) => ReactNode;
11 }
12 
13 export const Slot = ({
14   index,
15   as: Tag = 'li',
16   className,
17   children,
18 }: SlotProps) => {
19   const {
20     renderProps,
21     outerRef,
22     slotRef,
23     dragRef,
24     handleClick,
25     pointerHandlers,
26     isBeingDragged,
27   } = useSlotBehavior(index);
28 
29   const {
30     label,
31     isEmpty,
32     isWrong,
33     showCursor,
34     isPreview,
35     previewLabel,
36   } = renderProps;
37 
38   // When the slot is used inline (as="span", e.g. SentenceWithGaps inside <p>),
39   // the inner visual element must also be a <span> to avoid invalid
40   // block-in-inline HTML nesting. For list/block contexts, <div> is correct.
41   const InnerTag = Tag === 'span' ? 'span' : 'div';
42 
43   const ariaLabel = isEmpty
44     ? `Slot ${index + 1}, empty`
45     : `Slot ${index + 1}, filled with ${label}`;
46 
47   const stateClasses = [
48     'relative flex items-center justify-center border-2 transition-all overflow-hidden',
49     isPreview
50       ? 'border-dashed border-primary'
51       : isEmpty
52         ? 'border-border'
53         : isWrong
54           ? 'border-destructive bg-destructive/10 text-destructive'
55           : 'border-primary bg-primary/10 text-primary',
56     // Focus ring on active slot (driven by showCursor, works in all modes)
57     showCursor && isWrong
58       ? 'ring-2 ring-destructive ring-offset-2'
59       : showCursor
60         ? 'border-primary ring-2 ring-primary ring-offset-2'
61         : '',
62   ]
63     .filter(Boolean)
64     .join(' ');
65 
66   return (
67     // Outer wrapper: the drop target. p-1.5 expands the hit area ~6 px beyond
68     // the visual slot on all sides (pragmatic-board card pattern). data-zone-index
69     // here ensures touch elementsFromPoint also covers the expanded region.
70     <Tag
71       ref={
72         outerRef as Ref<
73           HTMLLIElement & HTMLSpanElement & HTMLDivElement
74         >
75       }
76       aria-label={ariaLabel}
77       data-zone-index={index}
78       className="relative p-1.5"
79     >
80       {/* Inner visual slot: carries the state-dependent styling. */}
81       <InnerTag
82         ref={slotRef as Ref<HTMLDivElement>}
83         className={[stateClasses, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}
84         style={
85           isPreview
86             ? { animation: 'pulse-ring 1.5s ease-in-out infinite' }
87             : undefined
88         }
89       >
90         {isEmpty ? (
91           <>
92             {isPreview && previewLabel !== null ? (
93               <span className="text-xl font-bold opacity-50">
94                 {previewLabel}
95               </span>
96             ) : (
97               children(renderProps)
98             )}
99             {showCursor ? (
100               <span
101                 aria-hidden="true"
102                 className="pointer-events-none absolute bottom-2 left-1/2 h-0.5 w-7 -translate-x-1/2 rounded-sm bg-primary animate-blink"
103               />
104             ) : null}
105           </>
106         ) : (
107           <>
108             <div
109               className="absolute inset-0 bg-muted/60 shadow-inner"
110               aria-hidden="true"
111             />
112             <button
113               ref={dragRef}
114               type="button"
115               className={`absolute inset-0 flex touch-none select-none cursor-grab items-center justify-center rounded-[inherit] text-card-foreground${isBeingDragged && !isPreview ? ' invisible pointer-events-none' : ''}${isPreview ? ' opacity-50' : ''}`}
116               aria-hidden={
117                 isBeingDragged && !isPreview ? 'true' : undefined
118               }
119               style={skeuoStyle}
120               onClick={handleClick}
121               onPointerDown={pointerHandlers.onPointerDown}
122               onPointerMove={pointerHandlers.onPointerMove}
123               onPointerUp={pointerHandlers.onPointerUp}
124               onPointerCancel={pointerHandlers.onPointerCancel}
125             >
126               {isPreview && previewLabel !== null ? (
127                 <span className="text-xl font-bold">
128                   {previewLabel}
129                 </span>
130               ) : (
131                 children(renderProps)
132               )}
133             </button>
134             {showCursor ? (
135               <span
136                 aria-hidden="true"
137                 className="pointer-events-none absolute bottom-2 left-1/2 h-0.5 w-7 -translate-x-1/2 rounded-sm bg-destructive animate-blink"
138               />
139             ) : null}
140           </>
141         )}
142       </InnerTag>
143     </Tag>
144   );
145 };
146
```

</details></tool-use>

1 import { dropTargetForElements } from '@atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop/element/adapter';
2 import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef } from 'react';
3 import { useAnswerGameContext } from '../useAnswerGameContext';
4 import { useAnswerGameDispatch } from '../useAnswerGameDispatch';
5 import { useSlotTileDrag } from '../useSlotTileDrag';
6 import { useTileEvaluation } from '../useTileEvaluation';
7 import {
8   triggerEjectReturn,
9   triggerPop,
10   triggerShake,
11 } from './slot-animations';
12 import type { RefObject } from 'react';
13 import { playSound } from '@/lib/audio/AudioFeedback';
14 
15 export interface SlotRenderProps {
16   label: string | null;
17   tileId: string | null;
18   isActive: boolean;
19   isWrong: boolean;
20   isLocked: boolean;
21   isEmpty: boolean;
22   showCursor: boolean;
23   isPreview: boolean;
24   previewLabel: string | null;
25 }
26 
27 export interface UseSlotBehaviorReturn {
28   renderProps: SlotRenderProps;
29   /** Outer wrapper — registered as drop target, carries data-zone-index. */
30   outerRef: RefObject<HTMLElement | null>;
31   /** Inner visual div — used for animations (shake, pop, eject). */
32   slotRef: RefObject<HTMLElement | null>;
33   dragRef: RefObject<HTMLButtonElement | null>;
34   handleClick: () => void;
35   isBeingDragged: boolean;
36   pointerHandlers: {
37     onPointerDown: (e: React.PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => void;
38     onPointerMove: (e: React.PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => void;
39     onPointerUp: (e: React.PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => void;
40     onPointerCancel: (e: React.PointerEvent<HTMLElement>) => void;
41   };
42 }
43 
44 export const useSlotBehavior = (
45   index: number,
46 ): UseSlotBehaviorReturn => {
47   const {
48     zones,
49     allTiles,
50     bankTileIds,
51     activeSlotIndex,
52     config,
53     dragActiveTileId,
54     dragHoverZoneIndex,
55   } = useAnswerGameContext();
56   const dispatch = useAnswerGameDispatch();
57   const { placeTile } = useTileEvaluation();
58 
59   const zone = zones[index];
60   const tileId = zone?.placedTileId ?? null;
61   const tile = tileId ? allTiles.find((t) => t.id === tileId) : null;
62   const label = tile?.label ?? null;
63   const isWrong = zone?.isWrong ?? false;
64   const isLocked = zone?.isLocked ?? false;
65   const isEmpty = tileId === null;
66 
67   // Resolve interaction mode: explicit config or inferred from inputMethod
68   const slotInteraction =
69     config.slotInteraction ??
70     (config.inputMethod === 'type' ? 'ordered' : 'free-swap');
71   const isOrdered = slotInteraction === 'ordered';
72 
73   const isActive = isOrdered && activeSlotIndex === index;
74   const showCursor =
75     activeSlotIndex === index &&
76     (isEmpty || isWrong) &&
77     config.inputMethod !== 'drag';
78   const isBeingDragged = tileId !== null && dragActiveTileId === tileId;
79 
80   // Preview derivation
81   const isPreviewTarget =
82     dragHoverZoneIndex === index && dragActiveTileId !== null;
83 
84   const draggedTile = dragActiveTileId
85     ? allTiles.find((t) => t.id === dragActiveTileId)
86     : null;
87 
88   let isPreview = false;
89   let previewLabel: string | null = null;
90 
91   if (isPreviewTarget) {
92     isPreview = true;
93     previewLabel = draggedTile?.label ?? null;
94   } else if (isBeingDragged && dragHoverZoneIndex !== null) {
95     // This slot is the source of the active drag AND the drag is hovering over some target.
96     isPreview = true;
97     // Source slot shows what it will receive: the target's current tile label.
98     const targetZone = zones[dragHoverZoneIndex];
99     const targetTile = targetZone?.placedTileId
100       ? allTiles.find((t) => t.id === targetZone.placedTileId)
101       : null;
102     previewLabel = targetTile?.label ?? null; // null means source will become empty
103   }
104 
105   const outerRef = useRef<HTMLElement | null>(null);
106   const slotRef = useRef<HTMLElement | null>(null);
107   const prevIsWrongRef = useRef(isWrong);
108   const prevTileIdRef = useRef(tileId);
109   const prevDragActiveTileIdRef = useRef(dragActiveTileId);
110   // Holds the startFade handle returned by triggerEjectReturn so we can call
111   // it when EJECT_TILE fires and the tile is back in the bank.
112   const startFadeRef = useRef<(() => void) | null>(null);
113 
114   // Drop target handler — choose evaluation or swap based on interaction mode
115   const handleDrop = useCallback(
116     (droppedTileId: string, targetZoneIndex: number) => {
117       const targetZone = zones[targetZoneIndex];
118       if (!targetZone) return;
119 
120       // Always check for slot-to-slot drag first (regardless of interaction
121       // mode) so that SWAP_TILES clears the source slot. Falling through to
122       // placeTile for slot-to-slot would leave the source unchanged and
123       // duplicate the tile.
124       const sourceZoneIndex = zones.findIndex(
125         (z) => z.placedTileId === droppedTileId,
126       );
127       if (sourceZoneIndex !== -1) {
128         const droppedTile = allTiles.find(
129           (t) => t.id === droppedTileId,
130         );
131         const displacedTile = targetZone.placedTileId
132           ? allTiles.find((t) => t.id === targetZone.placedTileId)
133           : null;
134         const sourceZone = zones[sourceZoneIndex];
135         const droppedCorrect =
136           droppedTile?.value === targetZone.expectedValue;
137 
138         if (displacedTile) {
139           // True swap: correct if either tile lands correctly
140           const displacedCorrect =
141             displacedTile.value === sourceZone?.expectedValue;
142           playSound(
143             droppedCorrect || displacedCorrect ? 'correct' : 'wrong',
144           );
145         } else {
146           // Move to empty slot: evaluate only the moved tile
147           playSound(droppedCorrect ? 'correct' : 'wrong');
148         }
149 
150         dispatch({
151           type: 'SWAP_TILES',
152           fromZoneIndex: sourceZoneIndex,
153           toZoneIndex: targetZoneIndex,
154         });
155         return;
156       }
157       placeTile(droppedTileId, targetZoneIndex);
158     },
159     [placeTile, zones, dispatch, allTiles],
160   );
161 
162   // Register outerRef (the wrapper element, p-1.5 larger than the visual slot)
163   // as the drop target so the hit area extends ~6 px beyond the visual slot on
164   // all sides. getIsSticky keeps the target active once the pointer enters it,
165   // matching the pragmatic-board card pattern.
166   useEffect(() => {
167     const el = outerRef.current;
168     if (!el) return;
169     return dropTargetForElements({
170       element: el,
171       getIsSticky: () => true,
172       getData: () => ({ zoneIndex: index }),
173       onDragEnter: () => {
174         dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER', zoneIndex: index });
175       },
176       onDragLeave: () => {
177         dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER', zoneIndex: null });
178       },
179       onDrop: ({ source }) => {
180         dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER', zoneIndex: null });
181         // Slot-tile drags (sourceZoneIndex present) handle their own
182         // REMOVE_TILE + SET_DRAG_ACTIVE sequence via useSlotTileDrag's onDrop.
183         if (typeof source.data['sourceZoneIndex'] === 'number') return;
184         const sourceTileId = source.data['tileId'];
185         if (typeof sourceTileId === 'string') {
186           handleDrop(sourceTileId, index);
187           // Clear drag active state here — the draggable component (bank tile)
188           // unmounts when PLACE_TILE fires, so its own onDrop may never run.
189           dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE', tileId: null });
190         }
191       },
192     });
193   }, [index, handleDrop, dispatch]);
194 
195   const handleHoverZone = useCallback(
196     (zoneIndex: number | null) => {
197       dispatch({ type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER', zoneIndex });
198     },
199     [dispatch],
200   );
201 
202   // Drag source for filled slots
203   const {
204     dragRef,
205     onPointerDown,
206     onPointerMove,
207     onPointerUp,
208     onPointerCancel,
209   } = useSlotTileDrag({
210     tileId,
211     label,
212     zoneIndex: index,
213     onDrop: handleDrop,
214     onHoverZone: handleHoverZone,
215   });
216 
217   // Click handler: remove tile from slot (returns to bank)
218   const handleClick = useCallback(() => {
219     if (isEmpty) return;
220     dispatch({ type: 'REMOVE_TILE', zoneIndex: index });
221   }, [isEmpty, dispatch, index]);
222 
223   // Feedback animations: trigger on state transitions
224   useEffect(() => {
225     const el = slotRef.current;
226     if (!el) return;
227 
228     const prevDragActiveTileId = prevDragActiveTileIdRef.current;
229     prevDragActiveTileIdRef.current = dragActiveTileId;
230 
231     const wasWrong = prevIsWrongRef.current;
232     prevIsWrongRef.current = isWrong;
233 
234     const prevTileId = prevTileIdRef.current;
235     prevTileIdRef.current = tileId;
236     const freshlyPlaced = tileId !== null && prevTileId === null;
237 
238     if (
239       tileId !== null &&
240       prevTileId !== null &&
241       tileId !== prevTileId
242     ) {
243       // Tile swapped between slots — animate per zone; sound played once in handleDrop.
244       if (isWrong) {
245         triggerShake(el);
246       } else {
247         triggerPop(el);
248       }
249     } else if (isWrong && !wasWrong) {
250       // Newly wrong via PLACE_TILE (sound already played by useTileEvaluation).
251       triggerShake(el);
252 
253       if (config.wrongTileBehavior === 'lock-auto-eject') {
254         // Start the eject animation after the shake settles.
255         const animationTimerId = setTimeout(() => {
256           const tileEl = dragRef.current;
257           if (!tileEl) return;
258           const { startFade } = triggerEjectReturn(
259             tileEl,
260             tileId,
261             () => {},
262           );
263           startFadeRef.current = startFade;
264         }, 350);
265         return () => clearTimeout(animationTimerId);
266       }
267     } else if (freshlyPlaced && !isWrong) {
268       // Correctly placed from bank (sound already played by useTileEvaluation).
269       triggerPop(el);
270     } else if (tileId === null && prevTileId !== null) {
271       // Tile left this slot.
272       // Suppress the sound only when the tile moved to another slot (swap/move),
273       // where handleDrop already played the sound. If the tile went back to the
274       // bank (REMOVE_TILE via drag), play 'tile-place' here.
275       const movedToSlot =
276         prevTileId === prevDragActiveTileId &&
277         !bankTileIds.includes(prevTileId);
278       if (!movedToSlot) {
279         playSound('tile-place');
280       }
281       startFadeRef.current?.();
282       startFadeRef.current = null;
283     }
284   }, [
285     isWrong,
286     tileId,
287     bankTileIds,
288     config.wrongTileBehavior,
289     dragRef,
290     dragActiveTileId,
291   ]);
292 
293   // EJECT_TILE dispatch lives in its own effect (no DOM ref needed) so the
294   // timer survives when the bank tile unmounts after PLACE_TILE. The animation
295   // effect above also uses isWrong/tileId so both fire on the same transition.
296   useEffect(() => {
297     if (!isWrong || config.wrongTileBehavior !== 'lock-auto-eject')
298       return;
299     const timerId = setTimeout(() => {
300       dispatch({ type: 'EJECT_TILE', zoneIndex: index });
301     }, 1000);
302     return () => clearTimeout(timerId);
303   }, [isWrong, config.wrongTileBehavior, dispatch, index]);
304 
305   return {
306     renderProps: {
307       label,
308       tileId,
309       isActive,
310       isWrong,
311       isLocked,
312       isEmpty,
313       showCursor,
314       isPreview,
315       previewLabel,
316     },
317     outerRef,
318     slotRef,
319     dragRef,
320     handleClick,
321     isBeingDragged,
322     pointerHandlers: {
323       onPointerDown,
324       onPointerMove,
325       onPointerUp,
326       onPointerCancel,
327     },
328   };
329 };
330
```

</details></tool-use>

### 3. **Type Definitions from `src/games/number-match/types.ts`**

Key types:

- **`NumberMatchRound`**: `{ value: number; objectImage?: string }`
- **`NumberMatchConfig extends AnswerGameConfig`**:
  - `component: 'NumberMatch'`
  - `mode: 'numeral-to-group' | 'group-to-numeral' | 'numeral-to-word' | 'word-to-numeral'`
  - `tileStyle: 'dots' | 'objects' | 'fingers'`
  - `range: { min: number; max: number }`
  - `rounds: NumberMatchRound[]`

Config fields include: `inputMethod`, `wrongTileBehavior`, `mode`, `tileStyle`, `tileBankMode`, `distractorCount`, `totalRounds`, `roundsInOrder`, `range` (min/max), `ttsEnabled`.

### 4. **NumberMatch.tsx Component Structure**

**Key Props**: `config: NumberMatchConfig`, `initialState?: AnswerGameDraftState`, `sessionId?: string`, `seed?: string`

**How it handles `allDice` (lines 126-132)**:

```typescript
const allDice = allTiles.every((t) => {
  const n = Number.parseInt(t.value, 10);
  return !Number.isNaN(n) && n <= 6;
});
const slotClass = allDice
  ? 'size-20 rounded-2xl'
  : 'h-[72px] w-32 rounded-2xl';
```

Checks if all tiles have numeric values ≤ 6 to determine slot sizing. Dice (1–6) get small squares; dominoes/larger tiles get wider slots.

**Slot Rendering (lines 154–177)**:

- Renders `<Slot>` components with a render callback `({ label })`
- Inside the callback, checks `tileStyle === 'dots'` and numeric value to decide:
  - If `n <= 6`: renders `<DiceFace value={n} />`
  - If `n > 6`: renders `<DominoTile value={n} />`
  - Otherwise: renders plain text `<span>{label}</span>`

**Mode Gating (lines 134–150)**:

- `showTextQuestion` is true for `'numeral-to-group'`, `'numeral-to-word'`, `'word-to-numeral'`
- Renders `<DotGroupQuestion>` for `'group-to-numeral'` mode
- Renders `<TextQuestion>` for other modes

**Tile Rendering**: All tile rendering is delegated to the slot callback — the component doesn't directly render `DiceFace` or `DominoTile`, only instructs slots how to render based on label parsing.

### 5. **NumeralTileBank.tsx Component Structure**

**Props**: `tileStyle: TileStyle` (`'dots' | 'objects' | 'fingers'`)

**How it handles dice vs. domino tiles (lines 9–13, 88–89)**:

```typescript
const getIsDomino = (tileStyle: TileStyle, numericValue: number) =>
  tileStyle === 'dots' &&
  !Number.isNaN(numericValue) &&
  numericValue > 6 &&
  numericValue <= 12;
```

Domino detection: only in `'dots'` mode, values 7–12.

**How it handles `allDice` branching**:

- Uses `useAnswerGameContext()` to access `allTiles`, `bankTileIds`, `dragActiveTileId`, `dragHoverBankTileId`
- For each tile, computes `isDomino` dynamically
- Applies different sizing: `isDomino ? 'h-[72px] w-32' : 'size-20'`

**Hover Previews (lines 187–192, 212–215)**:

- When `isHoverTarget` is true, renders a dashed border + semi-transparent preview
- Shows `tile.label` on empty holes being previewed

**Tile Bank Rendering (lines 160–219)**:

- Tiles in bank: renders `<NumeralTile>` wrapper with hole background
- Tiles in slots: shows empty hole with optional preview label when drag-hovering

**NumeralTile sub-component (lines 71–123)**:

- Uses `useDraggableTile()` for drag interaction
- Renders `<DiceFace>` for `n ≤ 6`, `<DominoTile>` for `7 ≤ n ≤ 12`, plain text otherwise
- Has size classes: `size-20` for dice, `h-[72px] w-32` for dominoes

### 6. **DotGroupQuestion.tsx Component Structure**

**Props**: `count: number`, `prompt: string`

**Current Rendering (lines 21–27)**:

```typescript
{Array.from({ length: count }, (_, i) => (
  <span
    key={i}
    role="presentation"
    className="size-10 rounded-full bg-primary"
  />
))}
```

Simple circle dots (`size-10 rounded-full bg-primary`). Wrapped in a button with `useGameTTS()` for audio playback. Renders flex-wrapped dots centered.

### 7. **build-numeral-round.ts — Round Generation**

**Function**: `buildNumeralRound(value: number, bank: NumeralBankOptions)`

**Returns**: `{ tiles: TileItem[]; zones: AnswerZone[] }`

**Labels Used**:

- Single zone with `expectedValue: String(value)`
- Tiles created with `label: String(n)`, `value: String(n)` for each numeral
- Only numeric string labels (`'1'`, `'2'`, etc.)

**How it generates**:

1. Creates one zone (answer slot) with `expectedValue = String(value)`
2. Builds numeral array: `[value]` + distractors (if `tileBankMode === 'distractors'`)
3. Shuffles and maps to `TileItem[]` with numeric labels and values
4. Returns both arrays

### 8. **Story Files in number-match Directory**

**NumberMatch.stories.tsx**:

- 4 stories: `NumeralToGroup`, `GroupToNumeral`, `NumeralToWord`, `WordToNumeral`
- Each tests different `mode` combinations with various `tileStyle` values

**NumeralTileBank.stories.tsx**:

- 4 stories: `Default` (dots), `FingersStyle`, `ObjectsStyle`, `DragHoverBankTile`
- Uses `AnswerGameProvider` with mock tiles (1–5) and zones

**DotGroupQuestion.stories.tsx**:

- 3 stories: `OneDot`, `ThreeDots`, `FiveDots`
- Tests different dot counts with TTS integration

### 9. **DiceFace and DominoTile Components**

**Location**: Exported from `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/match-number-improvements/src/games/number-match/NumeralTileBank/NumeralTileBank.tsx` (lines 35–69)

**DiceFace Component**:

```typescript
const DiceFace = ({ value }: { value: number }) => {
  const pips = DICE_PIPS[value] ?? [];
  return (
    <div className="grid grid-cols-3 grid-rows-3 gap-1 p-1" aria-hidden="true">
      {/* 9-cell grid, filled/empty based on pip positions */}
    </div>
  );
};
```

- Uses `DICE_PIPS` lookup table (positions 0–8 for each die value 1–6)
- Renders 3×3 grid of dots, fills pip positions based on die value
- Supports: 1 (center), 2 (corners), 3 (diagonal), 4 (four corners), 5 (five including center), 6 (all but center cross)

**DominoTile Component**:

```typescript
const DominoTile = ({ value }: { value: number }) => {
  const [left, right] = DOMINO_SPLIT[value] ?? [6, value - 6];
  return (
    <div className="flex items-center gap-0" aria-hidden="true">
      <DiceFace value={left} />
      <span className="h-10 w-px shrink-0 bg-current opacity-30" />
      <DiceFace value={right} />
    </div>
  );
};
```

- Uses `DOMINO_SPLIT` to decompose values 7–12 into two die values
- Renders two `DiceFace` components side-by-side with a vertical divider
- Domino splits: 7→[4,3], 8→[4,4], 9→[5,4], 10→[6,4], 11→[6,5], 12→[6,6]

**Exports**: Both are named exports from `NumeralTileBank.tsx` (lines 8 in NumberMatch.tsx imports them)

### 10. **Slot Component and previewLabel Logic**

**Location**: `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/match-number-improvements/src/components/answer-game/Slot/Slot.tsx` and `useSlotBehavior.ts`

**Slot.tsx** (lines 13–145):

- Takes `children: (props: SlotRenderProps) => ReactNode` render callback
- Receives `renderProps` from `useSlotBehavior()` which includes:
  - `label: string | null` — the placed tile's label
  - `previewLabel: string | null` — what the slot will receive on drop
  - `isPreview: boolean` — true if dragging over or dragging from this slot

**previewLabel Logic** (useSlotBehavior.ts, lines 80–103):

```typescript
let isPreview = false;
let previewLabel: string | null = null;

if (isPreviewTarget) {
  // Slot is the drop target — show the dragged tile's label
  isPreview = true;
  previewLabel = draggedTile?.label ?? null;
} else if (isBeingDragged && dragHoverZoneIndex !== null) {
  // Slot is the source — show what it will receive (target's tile)
  isPreview = true;
  const targetZone = zones[dragHoverZoneIndex];
  const targetTile = targetZone?.placedTileId ? ... : null;
  previewLabel = targetTile?.label ?? null; // null means slot becomes empty
}
```

**Slot Rendering with previewLabel** (Slot.tsx, lines 90–141):

- If `isEmpty && isPreview && previewLabel !== null`: renders `<span className="text-xl font-bold opacity-50">{previewLabel}</span>` (semi-transparent preview)
- If `!isEmpty`: renders a button with placed tile, and if `isPreview && previewLabel !== null`: overrides with preview label at opacity-50 on drag-over
- The `children` render callback is skipped when previewing — slot shows the preview label instead

**Key: NumberMatch.tsx never directly uses `previewLabel`** — it only passes the slot's render callback `({ label })` which reads the placed tile's label, not the preview. The preview is handled entirely by Slot's internal logic and renders before the callback.

---

## Summary

This is a comprehensive answer-game system for matching numerals to groups (dots/dominoes), with:

- **Type-safe config** for 4 game modes and 3 visual styles
- **Smart tile rendering**: DiceFace for 1–6, DominoTile for 7–12, fallback to text
- **Drag-and-drop**: With preview labels showing what will land on drop
- **DotGroupQuestion**: Simple circular dot renderer for visual matching prompts
- **Slot-based answer zones**: Support for free-swap and ordered interactions
- **TTS integration**: Voice prompts for accessibility

All file paths are absolute and ready for implementation reference.

# Domino Tile Overhaul Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use
> superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or
> superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use
> checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Unify all `dots`-style tiles into a single vertical domino shape,
replace the current `DiceFace`/`DominoTile` split with a single `DominoTile`
component, update the `DOMINO_SPLIT` map for young-reader-friendly splits,
and remove `allDice` branching logic.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, React, Tailwind CSS

**Spec:**
[docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-10-match-number-improvements-design.md](../specs/2026-04-10-match-number-improvements-design.md)
(Section 1 — Domino Tile Overhaul)

---

## File Map

### New files

_(none)_

### Modified files

- `src/games/number-match/NumeralTileBank/NumeralTileBank.tsx` — rewrite
  `DominoTile` as a vertical layout, make `DiceFace` internal-only, update
  `DOMINO_SPLIT` map, unify tile sizing, remove `getIsDomino` branching
- `src/games/number-match/NumberMatch/NumberMatch.tsx` — remove `allDice`
  branching, unify slot sizing for dots mode, update slot render callback to
  always use `DominoTile`

---

## Steps

- [ ] **Step 1 — Update `DOMINO_SPLIT` map**
  - File: `NumeralTileBank.tsx`
  - Replace the existing `DOMINO_SPLIT` with the new young-reader-friendly map
    (top/bottom, smaller value on top):

    | Value | Top | Bottom |
    | ----- | --- | ------ |
    | 1     | 1   | 0      |
    | 2     | 2   | 0      |
    | 3     | 3   | 0      |
    | 4     | 4   | 0      |
    | 5     | 5   | 0      |
    | 6     | 6   | 0      |
    | 7     | 2   | 5      |
    | 8     | 4   | 4      |
    | 9     | 4   | 5      |
    | 10    | 5   | 5      |
    | 11    | 5   | 6      |
    | 12    | 6   | 6      |

  - Keys 1-6 now have a bottom value of `0` (blank half)

- [ ] **Step 2 — Rewrite `DominoTile` to vertical orientation**
  - File: `NumeralTileBank.tsx`
  - Change layout from horizontal (`flex items-center`) to vertical
    (`flex flex-col items-center`)
  - Render top `DiceFace`, then a horizontal divider line, then bottom
    `DiceFace`
  - Divider: `<span className="h-px w-11 shrink-0 bg-current opacity-30" />`
  - Padding: `py-3` between pip grids and divider
  - Bottom value of `0` renders a blank half (empty `DiceFace` or empty space)
  - All tiles: `w-[72px] h-[136px]` (tall rectangle)

- [ ] **Step 3 — Make `DiceFace` internal, stop exporting it**
  - File: `NumeralTileBank.tsx`
  - Remove `DiceFace` from named exports
  - `DiceFace` stays as a private helper inside the file (renders one half of
    a domino)
  - Handle value `0` in `DiceFace` — render an empty 3x3 grid (no pips)

- [ ] **Step 4 — Remove `getIsDomino` helper and `allDice` branching in
  `NumeralTileBank`**
  - File: `NumeralTileBank.tsx`
  - Delete the `getIsDomino` function
  - When `tileStyle === 'dots'`, always use the same domino dimensions
    (`w-[72px] h-[136px]`) for tiles and holes regardless of value
  - Remove conditional sizing (`isDomino ? 'h-[72px] w-32' : 'size-20'`)
  - All tiles in dots mode render via `DominoTile`

- [ ] **Step 5 — Remove `allDice` branching in `NumberMatch.tsx`**
  - File: `NumberMatch.tsx`
  - Remove the `allDice` computed boolean and its slot class logic
  - When dots mode is active, all slots use portrait dimensions:
    `w-[72px] h-[136px] rounded-2xl`
  - Update slot render callback to always use `<DominoTile value={n} />`
    instead of branching on `n <= 6` vs `n > 6`

- [ ] **Step 6 — Update imports**
  - File: `NumberMatch.tsx`
  - Remove `DiceFace` import (no longer exported)
  - Keep `DominoTile` import

- [ ] **Step 7 — Verify**
  - `yarn typecheck` passes
  - `yarn lint` passes
  - `yarn test` passes (existing tests in
    `NumeralTileBank.test.tsx` and `NumberMatch.test.tsx`)
  - Storybook stories render correctly with uniform vertical tiles

```

```md
# Mode Bug Fix + New Modes Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use
> superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or
> superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use
> checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Fix the `group-to-numeral` rendering bug, introduce the
`tilesShowGroup` gate, expand the mode type to 8 variants, add word/ordinal
tile rendering, and update slot/bank sizing to match.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, React, Tailwind CSS

**Spec:**
[docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-10-match-number-improvements-design.md](../specs/2026-04-10-match-number-improvements-design.md)
(Section 2 — Mode Bug Fix + New Modes, excluding the number-words utility)

**Dependency:** The `number-words.ts` utility must be implemented first (see
[2026-04-10-number-words-utility.md](./2026-04-10-number-words-utility.md)).

---

## File Map

### New files

_(none)_

### Modified files

- `src/games/number-match/types.ts` — expand `NumberMatchMode` to 8 variants,
  update `NumberMatchConfig` field
- `src/games/number-match/build-numeral-round.ts` — generate display labels
  for word/ordinal modes using the number-words utility
- `src/games/number-match/NumberMatch/NumberMatch.tsx` — add `tilesShowGroup`
  gate, update slot dimensions, update rendering logic for word tiles
- `src/games/number-match/NumeralTileBank/NumeralTileBank.tsx` — accept
  `tilesShowGroup` prop, gate domino rendering, update NumeralTile to render
  word tiles
- `src/games/number-match/NumberMatch/NumberMatch.stories.tsx` — add stories
  for all 8 modes
- `src/games/number-match/NumeralTileBank/NumeralTileBank.stories.tsx` —
  update stories for new tile types

---

## Steps

- [ ] **Step 1 — Expand `NumberMatchMode` type**
  - File: `types.ts`
  - Replace the current mode union with all 8 variants:

    \```typescript
    type NumberMatchMode =
      | 'numeral-to-group'
      | 'group-to-numeral'
      | 'cardinal-number-to-text'
      | 'cardinal-text-to-number'
      | 'ordinal-number-to-text'
      | 'ordinal-text-to-number'
      | 'cardinal-to-ordinal'
      | 'ordinal-to-cardinal';
    \```

  - Remove old `'numeral-to-word'` and `'word-to-numeral'` variants

- [ ] **Step 2 — Add `tilesShowGroup` gate in `NumberMatch.tsx`**
  - File: `NumberMatch.tsx`
  - Derive `tilesShowGroup`:
    `const tilesShowGroup = config.mode === 'numeral-to-group';`
  - Pass `tilesShowGroup` as a prop to `NumeralTileBank`
  - Domino rendering only applies when
    `tilesShowGroup && config.tileStyle === 'dots'`

- [ ] **Step 3 — Update slot dimensions**
  - File: `NumberMatch.tsx`
  - Slot sizing logic:
    - `tilesShowGroup && tileStyle === 'dots'` -> portrait
      `w-[72px] h-[136px] rounded-2xl`
    - All other modes -> square `size-20 rounded-2xl`

- [ ] **Step 4 — Update slot render callback**
  - File: `NumberMatch.tsx`
  - When `tilesShowGroup && tileStyle === 'dots'` -> render
    `<DominoTile value={n} />`
  - When mode is a word/ordinal mode -> render text label from tile
  - Otherwise -> render plain numeral text

- [ ] **Step 5 — Update question rendering for new modes**
  - File: `NumberMatch.tsx`
  - `group-to-numeral` -> `<DotGroupQuestion>`
  - `numeral-to-group` -> `<TextQuestion>` (shows numeral)
  - Cardinal/ordinal modes -> `<TextQuestion>` showing the appropriate
    question text:
    - `cardinal-number-to-text`: show numeral (e.g. "7")
    - `cardinal-text-to-number`: show cardinal word (e.g. "seven")
    - `ordinal-number-to-text`: show ordinal number (e.g. "7th")
    - `ordinal-text-to-number`: show ordinal word (e.g. "seventh")
    - `cardinal-to-ordinal`: show cardinal word (e.g. "seven")
    - `ordinal-to-cardinal`: show ordinal word (e.g. "seventh")
  - Use `toCardinalText`, `toOrdinalText`, `toOrdinalNumber` from
    `number-words.ts` for conversions
  - Locale from `useParams({ from: '/$locale' })`

- [ ] **Step 6 — Accept `tilesShowGroup` prop in `NumeralTileBank`**
  - File: `NumeralTileBank.tsx`
  - Add `tilesShowGroup: boolean` to props
  - Gate domino rendering: only render `DominoTile` when
    `tilesShowGroup && tileStyle === 'dots'`
  - When `!tilesShowGroup`: render plain text tile (numeral or word)
  - Tile sizing: portrait for dots+group, square otherwise

- [ ] **Step 7 — Add word tile UI in `NumeralTile`**
  - File: `NumeralTileBank.tsx`
  - Word tiles: square with `min-w-[80px] h-[80px]`
  - Font size scales down for longer words (e.g. `text-sm` for > 6 chars)
  - Long words with hyphens break across two lines (`hyphens-auto`)
  - Slots and bank holes use matching `min-w-[80px]`

- [ ] **Step 8 — Update `build-numeral-round.ts` for word labels**
  - File: `build-numeral-round.ts`
  - Accept `mode` and `locale` parameters
  - Generate tile labels based on mode:
    - `numeral-to-group` / `group-to-numeral`: numeric labels (current)
    - `cardinal-number-to-text`: answer tiles show cardinal words
    - `cardinal-text-to-number`: answer tiles show numerals
    - `ordinal-number-to-text`: answer tiles show ordinal words
    - `ordinal-text-to-number`: answer tiles show numerals with ordinal suffix
    - `cardinal-to-ordinal`: answer tiles show ordinal words
    - `ordinal-to-cardinal`: answer tiles show cardinal words
  - Matching always compares underlying numeric `value`, not text

- [ ] **Step 9 — Update stories**
  - File: `NumberMatch.stories.tsx`
  - Add stories for all 8 modes: `NumeralToGroup`, `GroupToNumeral`,
    `CardinalNumberToText`, `CardinalTextToNumber`, `OrdinalNumberToText`,
    `OrdinalTextToNumber`, `CardinalToOrdinal`, `OrdinalToCardinal`
  - File: `NumeralTileBank.stories.tsx`
  - Add story for word-style tiles

- [ ] **Step 10 — Verify**
  - `yarn typecheck` passes
  - `yarn lint` passes
  - `yarn test` passes
  - All 8 mode stories render correctly in Storybook

```

```md
# Countable Dots Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use
> superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or
> superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use
> checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Make each dot in `DotGroupQuestion` individually tappable so kids
can count them one by one, with per-dot numbering and TTS on each tap.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, React, Tailwind CSS, Web Speech API (TTS)

**Spec:**
[docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-10-match-number-improvements-design.md](../specs/2026-04-10-match-number-improvements-design.md)
(Section 3 — Countable Dots)

---

## File Map

### New files

_(none)_

### Modified files

- `src/components/questions/DotGroupQuestion/DotGroupQuestion.tsx` — add
  per-dot tap state, numbering overlay, TTS on tap, auto-reset on count
  change
- `src/components/questions/DotGroupQuestion/DotGroupQuestion.test.tsx` —
  tests for tap-to-count, sequential numbering, reset on count change
- `src/components/questions/DotGroupQuestion/DotGroupQuestion.stories.tsx` —
  update stories to demonstrate countable dots

---

## Steps

- [ ] **Step 1 — Add per-dot tap state**
  - File: `DotGroupQuestion.tsx`
  - Add `useState<(number | null)[]>` initialised to
    `Array(count).fill(null)` — tracks assigned count per dot
  - Add a `useRef<number>` for `nextCount` starting at `1`
  - Reset both when `count` changes (use `useEffect` with `count` dependency)

- [ ] **Step 2 — Convert dots from `<span>` to `<button>`**
  - File: `DotGroupQuestion.tsx`
  - Replace each `<span role="presentation">` dot with a
    `<button type="button">`
  - Keep existing styling: `size-10 rounded-full bg-primary`
  - Add `aria-label` for accessibility (e.g. "Dot 3 of 7")
  - Add `onClick` handler for the tap logic

- [ ] **Step 3 — Implement tap handler**
  - File: `DotGroupQuestion.tsx`
  - On tap of an unnumbered dot (value is `null`):
    - Assign `nextCount.current` to that dot's position in the state array
    - Increment `nextCount.current`
  - Already-tapped dots do nothing on re-tap (guard: if value !== null, return)

- [ ] **Step 4 — Render number overlay on tapped dots**
  - File: `DotGroupQuestion.tsx`
  - When a dot has an assigned number, render the number centered on the dot
  - White text (`text-white`), bold, centered over the primary circle
  - Use `relative` positioning on the button with an `absolute` number overlay
  - Font size: `text-sm font-bold`

- [ ] **Step 5 — Add TTS on tap**
  - File: `DotGroupQuestion.tsx`
  - On each tap, speak the cardinal word for the assigned number
    (e.g. "one", "two", "three")
  - Use the existing `useGameTTS()` hook or `speechSynthesis.speak()` directly
  - Use `toCardinalText` from `number-words.ts` for locale-aware words
  - Locale sourced from existing context/params

- [ ] **Step 6 — Auto-reset on count change**
  - File: `DotGroupQuestion.tsx`
  - `useEffect` with `[count]` dependency:
    - Reset state array to `Array(count).fill(null)`
    - Reset `nextCount.current` to `1`
  - This handles round transitions

- [ ] **Step 7 — Update tests**
  - File: `DotGroupQuestion.test.tsx`
  - Test: tapping a dot assigns sequential numbers (1, 2, 3...)
  - Test: tapping an already-numbered dot does nothing
  - Test: state resets when `count` prop changes
  - Test: dots render as buttons with correct aria-labels

- [ ] **Step 8 — Update stories**
  - File: `DotGroupQuestion.stories.tsx`
  - Update existing stories to show interactive countable dots
  - Add a story with a higher count (e.g. 9 dots) to show wrapping

- [ ] **Step 9 — Verify**
  - `yarn typecheck` passes
  - `yarn lint` passes
  - `yarn test` passes

```

# Hover Preview Fix Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use
> superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or
> superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use
> checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Fix hover previews in slots and bank holes so they render a domino
face (when in dots+group mode) or the correct text label instead of always
showing plain text.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, React, Tailwind CSS

**Spec:**
[docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-10-match-number-improvements-design.md](../specs/2026-04-10-match-number-improvements-design.md)
(Section 4 — Hover Preview Fix)

**Dependency:** Requires the Domino Tile Overhaul
([2026-04-10-domino-tile-overhaul.md](./2026-04-10-domino-tile-overhaul.md))
and Mode Bug Fix + New Modes
([2026-04-10-mode-bugfix-new-modes.md](./2026-04-10-mode-bugfix-new-modes.md))
to be implemented first, since this plan builds on `tilesShowGroup` and the
new `DominoTile` component.

---

## File Map

### New files

_(none)_

### Modified files

- `src/games/number-match/NumberMatch/NumberMatch.tsx` — update slot render
  callback to render domino or text for `previewLabel`
- `src/games/number-match/NumeralTileBank/NumeralTileBank.tsx` — update bank
  hole hover preview to render domino or text

---

## Steps

- [ ] **Step 1 — Update slot preview rendering in `NumberMatch.tsx`**
  - File: `NumberMatch.tsx`
  - In the `Slot` render callback, handle the `previewLabel` case:
    - When `isPreview && previewLabel !== null`:
      - If `tilesShowGroup && tileStyle === 'dots'`: parse `previewLabel` as
        number, render `<DominoTile value={n} />` at `opacity-50`
      - Otherwise: render `<span>{previewLabel}</span>` at `opacity-50`
  - This requires the `Slot` render callback to receive `previewLabel` and
    `isPreview` in its render props (check `SlotRenderProps` — these are
    already available: `label`, `previewLabel`, `isPreview`)

- [ ] **Step 2 — Update bank hole hover preview in `NumeralTileBank.tsx`**
  - File: `NumeralTileBank.tsx`
  - When a bank hole is the hover target (`isHoverTarget`), the empty hole
    currently renders `tile.label` as plain text
  - Update to check `tilesShowGroup && tileStyle === 'dots'`:
    - If true: render `<DominoTile value={n} />` at `opacity-50` inside the
      hole
    - If false: render `<span>{tile.label}</span>` at `opacity-50` (current
      behavior)

- [ ] **Step 3 — Verify preview sizing**
  - Slot preview: domino preview should fit within the portrait slot
    (`w-[72px] h-[136px]`)
  - Bank hole preview: domino preview should fit within the bank hole
    dimensions
  - Word tile previews: text should fit within `min-w-[80px] h-[80px]` holes
  - Both previews use `opacity-50` for the semi-transparent effect

- [ ] **Step 4 — Test in Storybook**
  - Verify in `NumberMatch` stories:
    - `NumeralToGroup` mode: dragging a domino tile over a slot shows a
      semi-transparent domino preview
    - `GroupToNumeral` mode: dragging a numeral tile shows plain text preview
    - Word modes: dragging a word tile shows the word text as preview
  - Verify in `NumeralTileBank` stories:
    - Returning a tile to bank shows correct preview in the hole

- [ ] **Step 5 — Verify**
  - `yarn typecheck` passes
  - `yarn lint` passes
  - `yarn test` passes

```
